etymology.description: 大(大) (dà, "big; great") + 學(學) (xué, "study; learning") = "university" or "college" as a place of higher education
etymology.interpretation: The word 大學 (dàxué) refers to a place for advanced learning. The combination implies an institution for large-scale or advanced study and research, reflecting its role in higher education. Historically, 大學 (dàxué) is also a reference to a Confucian text, reinforcing the significance of profound learning and virtue in Chinese culture.

examples.chinese: 他在大学学习(他在大學學習)。
我梦想进入大学深造(我夢想進入大學深造)。
examples.english: I dream of furthering my studies at the university.

examples.chinese: 她在大学里结识了很多朋友(她在大學裡結識了很多朋友)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài dàxué lǐ jiéshí le hěn duō péngyǒu.
examples.english: She made many friends at the university.
